round2_improvement_notes.linguistic: Agent provided no output in either round; no improvement possible.
round2_improvement_notes.landscape: Agent acknowledged other agents' evidence in R2 and refined the reasoning to specifically target the US West Coast, though the final prediction remained the same.
round2_improvement_notes.botanics: Agent synthesized cross-domain evidence (regulatory/meta) in R2 to strengthen the US West Coast hypothesis, but the core botanical evidence remained unchanged.
round2_improvement_notes.regulatory: Agent used botanical/landscape evidence to downgrade the Canada candidate in R2, showing improved synthesis.
round2_improvement_notes.meta: Agent incorporated the blue curb and landscaping evidence in R2 to refine the location to California, improving specificity.

role_leakage_notes.botanics: Agent referenced 'regulatory and meta evidence' (blue curbs, trash bins) in R2 to support its botanical claims.
role_leakage_notes.regulatory: Agent referenced 'botanical and landscape evidence' in R2 to rule out Canada.
role_leakage_notes.meta: Agent referenced 'blue curb marking' (regulatory) and 'Mediterranean-style landscaping' (landscape) in R2.

verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge correctly synthesized the strong consensus from the landscape, botanics, regulatory, and meta agents. It explicitly noted the linguistic agent's lack of evidence and correctly used the cross-domain agreement (yellow lines, right-hand traffic, specific trash bins, Mediterranean flora) to confirm the US West Coast location.
